text_preview:
NCEP’s UNIFIED POST NCEP’s UNIFIED POST PROCESSOR (UPP)PROCESSOR (UPP)Hui-Ya Chuang
Presented by Matthew Pyle
OutlineOutline–Introduction–Components and Functions–Sample fields generated–Installation–Running wrfpost•Controlling output generation–Running copygb•Specifying target grid–Visualization
The critical big picture overviewThe critical big picture overview•The WRF post package (WPP) generates output in GRIB.•Processes model output from both the NMM and the ARW dynamical cores.•The WPP enables product generation on any output grid.
WPP – I/OWPP – I/O•The WRF post reads in model output in either binary or netCDF format using the WRF I/O API.•Users are encouraged to use netCDF formatted model output for simplicity.  NCEP uses binary output for speed.
Components of the WPPComponents of the WPP
wrfout Fileswrfpostcopygb(primarily ARW)
The WPP has two components:  wrfpost and copygb.
wrf_cntrl.parmVisualization

catalog_entry.summary: This document describes NCEP's Unified Post Processor (UPP) and its role in generating output from WRF model runs, including the use of GRIB format for data exchange.
